content.text: This plan has been prepared to ensure the safety of employees, minimize loss of life and property in emergency situations (fire, earthquake, explosion, chemical accidents, occupational accidents, etc.) that may occur at {{firma_adi}} workplace.
This plan covers all employees, visitors, subcontractor employees and all persons present at the workplace within {{firma_adi}}.
Occupational Health and Safety Law No. 6331
Regulation on Emergency Situations in Workplaces
Regulation on Fire Protection of Buildings
All employees calmly proceed to the nearest emergency exit.
Team leaders conduct roll call at the assembly area.
If personnel are missing, the emergency coordinator is immediately notified.
Emergency teams move to their assigned positions.
Wait in the assembly area until the situation becomes safe.
To determine the response and evacuation procedures to be applied in emergency situations such as fire, earthquake, explosion, chemical leakage.
Covers all emergency scenarios in the workplace and all personnel.
Emergency plan is prepared and kept up to date.
Emergency teams (fire suppression, rescue, protection, first aid) are designated.
Assignment training is provided to teams.
When an emergency is detected, the alarm system is activated.
Evacuation procedure is initiated and personnel are directed to the assembly point.
Roll call is taken and missing personnel are reported.
Evacuation drill is conducted at least once a year.
Drill results are evaluated and the plan is updated.
Emergency Coordinator: General coordination and decision making
Team leaders: Response and evacuation in their areas of responsibility
OHS Specialist: Plan preparation, training and drill coordination
All employees: Compliance with instructions and participation in drills
Law No. 6331 Articles 11-12 and Regulation on Emergency Situations in Workplaces

acil-durum-prosedur.description: General procedure to be applied in emergency situations
acil-durum-prosedur.title: Emergency Procedure

content.text: This report has been prepared in accordance with the Occupational Health and Safety Law No. 6331 and the Occupational Health and Safety Risk Assessment Regulation. It has been prepared for the purpose of identifying existing and potential hazards in the workplace, evaluating risks, and planning control measures.
The risk assessment has been carried out by the following team:
The following methods were used in the risk assessment:
Detailed risk analysis table is presented in ANNEX-1.

As a result of the inspections conducted at the workplace, the identified hazards and risks have been evaluated, and control measures have been recommended for each risk. Immediate action is required for high-risk areas.
Corrective activities for high-risk areas should be initiated urgently.
All employees should be provided with informational training about the risk assessment results.
Risk assessment should be renewed at least once a year.
In case of work accident or occupational disease, risk assessment should be updated and renewed.

risk-raporu.description: Risk assessment report under OHS Law No. 6331
risk-raporu.title: Risk Assessment Report
